prose: **Ethylene HOMO–LUMO gap** computed via DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') using PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: read from user-supplied ethylene.xyz file without further optimization; single point energy/orbitals calculated at the input geometry. Main result: HOMO–LUMO gap = **7.4357 eV** (electronic energy minimum). SCF converged in 6 iterations. A Koopmans-style evaluation yielded vertical ionization potential (IP) of 7.6156 eV and electron affinity (EA) of 0.179865 eV. No warnings were reported, and integrity checks confirmed results are trustworthy (SCF convergence achieved with a finite energy). Calculation command was: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --solvent none --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz`
